methods.description: Analysis of bee mortality data from acute toxicity tests using non-parametric statistical methods due to non-normal distribution of data.

steps.description: The percentage of dead bees in each cage was determined and each cage was considered as a replicate.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate percentage of dead bees per cage
outputs: percentage of dead bees per cage

steps.description: The effect of pesticide and pollen treatments on bee mortality was analysed using Kruskal-Wallis, followed by Dunn's multiple comparison tests with the Benjamini-Hochberg correction.
steps.searchable_summary: perform Kruskal-Wallis and Dunn's tests with Benjamini-Hochberg correction

steps.description: The epsilon squared (ε 2 ) was computed to obtain a measure of effect size between experimental groups.
steps.searchable_summary: compute epsilon squared for effect size

methods.description: Analysis of survival data from chronic toxicity tests using Cox proportional hazards regression model.

steps.description: Data were transformed in a survival table and the remaining bees were considered alive at day 16.
Survival data were analysed with a Cox proportional hazards regression model.
steps.searchable_summary: perform Cox proportional hazards regression analysis

methods.description: Analysis of the influence of experimental treatments on syrup consumption, vitellogenin expression, and pesticide detoxification using non-parametric statistical methods.

steps.description: The influence of experimental treatments on the cumulated syrup consumption, vitellogenin expression level and pesticide detoxification was analysed using Kruskal-Wallis, followed by Dunn's multiple comparison tests with the Benjamini-Hochberg correction.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze treatment effects on syrup consumption, vitellogenin expression, and detoxification
